'''Paul Rauhihi''' (born 3 July 1973) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who represented [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. Rauhihi played in both the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) and [[Super League]] as a {{rlp|pr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Rauhihi was born in [[Porirua, New Zealand]]. He was an orphan and was adopted early in life.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 3 Ep 5 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-3-ep-5/S2339-580/M71744-423 |access-date=2023-05-16 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}}</ref>

==Playing career==

===New Zealand career===
Rauhihi's junior clubs were the Cottingham Tigers and St George club in [[Wellington]].<ref name=coffey>Coffey and Wood ''The Kiwis: 100 Years of International Rugby League'' {{ISBN|1-86971-090-8}}</ref> He then joined the [[New Zealand Navy]] and played for the [[Northcote Tigers]] and [[North Harbour Sea Eagles]] before becoming an [[Auckland Warriors]] junior in 1994.<ref name=Harbour>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1994'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1994. p.102</ref><ref name="League sensation rockets into dilemma with Warriors">{{cite web|url=https://kura.aucklandlibraries.govt.nz/digital/collection/akldpeople/id/74710|title=League sensation rockets into dilemma with Warriors|publisher=North Shore Times Advertiser|date=11 November 1994|pages=95–96|access-date=22 April 2024}}</ref>

Rauhihi was part of the Northcote side that won the Fox Memorial in 1994. After leaving the navy to concentrate on his rugby league career, Rauhihi played in 17 reserve grade games for the Warriors in 1996 and was a part of the Reserve Grade side that lost the 1997 grand final.<ref name="Rauhihi jumps ship for Warriors">{{cite web|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-17279856.html|title=Rauhihi jumps ship for Warriors|publisher=Sunday Star-Times - 8 September 1996|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

In 1998, he played for [[Taranaki rugby league team|Taranaki]] before signing with the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref name=p144>''New Zealand Rugby League Annual '98'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1998. p.144</ref>

===Australian career===
Rauhihi was released by Melbourne during the [[1999 NRL season]] to sign with [[Newcastle Knights]]. He made his first grade début against [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] at [[Energy Australia Stadium]] on 27 June 1999. The following year, he played in Newcastle's preliminary final loss against the [[Sydney Roosters]].

In 2001, he moved to the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]. In the same year, he played in Canterbury's 52-10 elimination final loss against [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]]. In the [[2002 NRL season]], Rauhihi played 23 games for Canterbury in what turned out to be one of the club's most turbulent. After going on a 16-game winning streak, it was discovered by the [[NRL]] that Canterbury had exceeded the [[NRL]]'s salary cap by $2 million over three years including undisclosed payments made to players. As a result, the [[NRL]] fined Canterbury $500,000 and stripped them of all their 37 competition points; this meant that the club would finish the 2002 season with the wooden spoon.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2010/04/22/history-of-nrl-salary-cap-breaches/|title=History of NRL Salary Cap breaches|website=NRL}}</ref>

Rauhihi then moved to the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], winning the club's player of the year award in 2003.<ref name="Honours">{{cite web|url=http://www.cowboys.com.au/default.aspx?s=honours|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111012233017/http://www.cowboys.com.au/default.aspx?s=honours|url-status=dead|archive-date=12 October 2011|title=Honours at cowboys.com.au|publisher=cowboys.com.au|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}}</ref> He also captained the side due to regular captain [[Paul Bowman (rugby league)|Paul Bowman]] being out with injury in 2004, and also when [[Travis Norton]] was out due to injury in 2005. He played at {{rlp|pr}} in the [[2005 NRL Grand Final]], the Cowboys' first, which they lost to the [[Wests Tigers]].

===English career===
Rauhihi moved to England after the 2005 grand final loss, joining the [[Warrington Wolves]] where he was given the number 10 jersey. In 2009, after a number of seasons plagued by back injury, Rauhihi retired.

==Representative career==
In 1996 Rauhihi played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] side that toured PNG and then competed in the [[Pacific Challenge Series]].<ref name=maori>{{cite book
| last =John Coffey, Bernie Wood
| title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
| publisher =Huia Publishers
| year = 2008
| pages =  292
| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
| isbn =9781869693312}}</ref><ref name="Team of the Century - Week 6 - Wellington Rugby League">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-4979-0-0-0&sID=253118|title=Team of the Century - Week 6 - Wellington Rugby League|publisher=sportingpulse.com rg|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018|archive-date=8 November 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121108111935/http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-4979-0-0-0&sID=253118|url-status=dead}}</ref> In 2000 he was part of the side in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]].

Between 2002 and 2005 he played in seventeen matches for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]], including captaining the side in the 2004 Tr-nations series due to an injury to [[Ruben Wiki]]. He was also part of the [[2005 Rugby League Tri Nations|2005 Tri Nations]] squad that won the competition.

== Match Fit ==
In 2023, Rauhihi participated in season 3 of ''Match Fit'', where former rugby players return to play against the Australian counterparts. He joined in the first season that featured former [[rugby league]] stars.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 3 Ep 1 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-3-ep-1/S2339-580/M70717-275 |access-date=2023-04-13 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}}</ref> On episode 5, he revealed that he was an orphan and was adopted, and he buried his debut Kiwis jersey with his adopted father when he died when Rauhihi was in his 20's. He also revealed that he prefers weights and power training over running.
